name: Parijata Tree
description: The Parijata tree, also known as Kalpavriksha or the Tree of Paradise, is a divine tree mentioned in Hindu mythology. It originated during the Samudra Manthan (churning of the ocean) and is considered one of the fourteen gems that emerged. The tree is renowned for its beautiful, fragrant flowers that are said to grant wishes and fulfill desires. It was initially possessed by Indra, the king of the gods, in his paradise, Indraloka. According to legend, the tree was later brought to Earth by Lord Krishna for his wife Satyabhama, who desired it after seeing its beauty in Indra's garden. The Parijata tree is a symbol of abundance, prosperity, and divine grace. Its flowers are often used in worship and are believed to bring good fortune. The tree represents the fulfillment of desires and the connection between the mortal and divine realms. The legend of the Parijata tree highlights themes of desire, jealousy, divine intervention, and the power of devotion. Its presence on Earth is a testament to the blessings bestowed upon humanity by the gods. The tree is also associated with eternal youth and beauty, further enhancing its significance in Hindu cosmology. The Parijata's fragrant blossoms are said to bloom only at night, adding to its mystical allure and making it a highly coveted treasure.
subtitle: The Wish-Granting Tree of Paradise
origin: Emerged from the Samudra Manthan (Churning of the Ocean)
currentLocation: Mythical gardens; believed to have a terrestrial descendant at Kintoor, Uttar Pradesh, India

keyMyths.title: Origin from Samudra Manthan
keyMyths.description: The Parijata tree arose during the churning of the ocean, a collaborative effort between gods and demons to obtain amrita (the nectar of immortality). It was one of the many treasures that emerged from the ocean depths.

keyMyths.title: Krishna and Satyabhama
keyMyths.description: Satyabhama, one of Krishna's wives, desired the Parijata tree after seeing it in Indra's garden. Krishna fought with Indra and eventually brought the tree to Earth for her, planting it in her garden.

sources.author: Veda Vyasa
sources.description: A major Hindu text that describes the story of Krishna obtaining the Parijata tree.
sources.text: A major Hindu text that describes the story of Krishna obtaining the Parijata tree.
